model.label: GLM-5
model.description: Z.AI • New-generation foundation model capable of providing reliable productivity in long-range agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Arizona enters as a legitimate national championship contender ranked 3rd in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite two-way profile—7th in adjusted offense and 3rd in adjusted defense—while Utah State sits approximately 27 spots lower at 29-30th in the major metrics, representing a substantial efficiency margin gap of roughly 10 points per 100 possessions. The Wildcats just won the Big 12 tournament championship with a victory over Houston and carry a nine-game winning streak, with star freshman Koa Peat having returned from his February injury to bolster an already dominant roster. Utah State's impressive Mountain West tournament championship and first-round win over Villanova show they're a quality team, but the Aggies haven't faced anything close to Arizona's elite defense all season, and the Wildcats' suffocating defense (91.1 adjusted defensive efficiency, 3rd nationally) should thoroughly stifle a Utah State offense that will be overwhelmed by the talent gap between Big 12 and Mountain West competition.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs Utah State Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated the substantial analytical gap between 1-seed Arizona and 9-seed Utah State, confirming Arizona's elite two-way profile, roster health with Koa Peat's return, and momentum from Big 12 championship against Utah State's solid but overmatched Mountain West profile.
evidence: Arizona ranked 3rd in both KenPom and Torvik with elite offense (7th) and defense (3rd), while Utah State sits 29-30th in major metrics
Arizona on 9-game winning streak after winning Big 12 tournament championship over Houston
Koa Peat returned from February injury and contributed in Big 12 tournament, restoring Arizona's full roster
Utah State won Mountain West tournament but faces massive step up in competition from MW to elite Big 12 opponent
Efficiency margin gap of approximately 10 points per 100 possessions favors Arizona significantly
